setting.note: The novel's reckoning happens in the Eastern Cape. A disgraced Cape Town academic retreats to his daughter's smallholding on the dry veld near Salem, between the old settler town of Grahamstown, now Makhanda, and the coast. The map anchors that country of hills, farm plots and a small-town animal clinic; the Cape Town scenes of his fall sit far to the west and are marked as excursions.

rights.originalWork: In copyright. J. M. Coetzee, first published 1999. This book is mapped as a cited work: we store none of its text and reproduce no passages. The places are facts and the notes are our own.
rights.textSource: None stored. A cited book reproduces no text.
rights.territoryNotes: Locations and original commentary only. No reproduction of the copyrighted text, in any territory.
rights.attribution: Disgrace, J. M. Coetzee (1999). Locations mapped by Read the Places; not affiliated with or endorsed by the author or publisher.

palette.note: The burnt ochre of the Eastern Cape earth the story turns on. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper.
